item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit, I'm a fan of the soup, but I'm also a preschool teacher and we use something similar to this in my classroom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Worry Hat is big mood

I could also use a worry hat

+1 for this wholesome read that is short and sweet too

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for the addendum alone. this kind of reminds me of that anomalous bowl of soup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wholesome SCP, it would do better to have better description and foundation-esque terminology as the others suggested, but I really like this one. Also maybe a discovery/history log.

+1 because it's wholesome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Concurring with the above, 'non-visual' and 'audible' are typical foundation usage, and typical technical usage would be to mention only the audible/physical effects, and not bring up the lack of visual effects unless the lack was specifically significant itself.

The Worry Hat is so wonderful that I feel less worried just having read about it. Thank you for writing it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Non sight based sensation is awfully unweildly and weird phrasing, and doesn't seem to fit the tone. That said, I like the concept. These sort of items that had been in innocent or casual use by some individual before inevitably leaving their hands and finding their way to foundation holding always interest me. The fact that it belonged to a teacher makes me imagine some almost Miss Frizzle situation, with a teacher so wonderful she almost seems like she's magic.

Kinda makes me wonder how hard the foundation would crack down on Frizzle if they found the stuff she was doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, I could really use a Worry Hat sometimes. But like, for life.

Thanks to Doctor Cimmerian here, and mikalhvi and evander-fucklear on Tumblr for the critique.

Picture is from the Wikimedia Commons.
